text: The east corridor is the widest corridor.
The new pharmacy is the most modern part.
The ICU is the most important ward.
The garden entrance is the quietest place.

Hi Graziele, this is Doctor Carter. Welcome back to Block 3. Here is a quick map of the hospital. Reception is the busiest area in the building. The ICU is the most important ward. The east corridor is the widest corridor. And the new pharmacy is the most modern part. Tomorrow you show a guest around. Use the -est words and the most. You will do great.

Hi Graziele, it is Ana. A quick tip before you show the guest around. To say something is number one, use the superlative. For short words like busy and wide, add -est: the busiest, the widest. For long words like important and modern, use the most: the most important, the most modern. Always put the before it. Keep it simple. You will do great.

Graziele shows a guest the hospital. First, the entrance: this is the main entrance. Then, the busiest area: reception is the busiest area. Next, the most important ward: the ICU is the most important ward. After that, the most modern part: the new pharmacy is the most modern part. Finally, the widest space: the east corridor is the widest corridor.
The east corridor is the widest corridor.
The ICU is the most important ward.
The new pharmacy is the most modern part.
The garden entrance is the quietest place.
